response: The internet originated in the 1960s with ARPANET, which sent its first message in 1969 and adopted TCP/IP in 1983, forming the basis of today’s network. Tim Berners‑Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web (HTML, URLs, HTTP) and the 1993 release of the Mosaic browser made the web usable for the public, sparking the dot‑com boom of the late‑1990s and later the rise of social media, mobile internet (post‑iPhone 2007), and cloud computing (AWS 2006). These developments have driven massive growth, and now over 5 billion people are online, with the internet underpinning commerce, communication, and entertainment worldwide.
